preview_text:
The model favors England at 53% win probability, with Croatia trailing at 18%. The underdog's chances are materially compressed, reflecting a clear asymmetry in the fixture despite the neutral venue.

A draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 29%, suggesting the match has genuine competitive tension rather than England running away early. The 35-percentage-point gap between an England win and a Croatia win indicates meaningful separation in central tendency, yet the draw probability is substantial enough to indicate this is not a one-sided affair. Competitive equilibrium is low; the model sees England as the clear favorite but does not price a blowout.

The sportsbook consensus runs 3 percentage points higher on England (56% vs 53%) and correspondingly lower on the draw (25% vs 29%). Polymarket prices align closely with books on the England win and draw. The gap between model and market is modest across all outcomes—well within typical forecast variance—suggesting broad agreement that England enters as favorite without exceptional divergence on match structure.
